title: Should I bother watching the original ep25 and 26
display_title: Should I bother watching the original ep25 and 26
wikitext:
[]: Should I bother watching the original ep25 and 26?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Thu Mar 18, 2010 11:09 pm I watched the entire series in one go up to Kaworu's death. Now, I had spoiled myself silly, so I knew about most of the original series already, save for the last two episodes. However, after hearing about how bad they were, I instead decided to watch EoE instead, since it's evidently the real ending, more or less. Years later, I'm wondering: should I bother with the last two?

[]:             Quote Post by Captain_Morgan » Thu Mar 18, 2010 11:17 pm You should at least see them once. And if you want the full uber complete ending, watch them with EoE...I'm sure Jimbo's got the precise time for when the two can be merged seamlessly written down somewhere.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Mar 18, 2010 11:32 pm Unfortunately, they can't be merged precisely, as various bits and pieces weave in and out of their respective timelines. However, I agree that you should watch them at least once. The mindfuck is a lot harder in EoTV than it is in EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Mar 18, 2010 11:50 pm Yes. Ignore what you've heard about them. Most hated them because they failed to resolve the narrative; that's what EoE is for. In terms of Anno's intentions with the series, the final two episodes are the most essential/important part of the series that goes right to the heart of the series' themes. If you're open to psychological/philosophical analysis you'll probably dig them. I love them and think they're TV at its most audacious, challenging, and artistically profound. Captain_Morgan wrote: I'm sure Jimbo's got the precise time for when the two can be merged seamlessly written down somewhere.

[]:             Quote Post by kirri » Fri Mar 19, 2010 12:00 am They're really good episodes, in my opinion. As far as a conclusive ending goes, I think EoE is better, but it doesn't get any more in-depth in to the character's psyches than episodes 25 and 26. Even though there was a lot of stuff left unresolved when I watched them the first time, I still thought it was an amazing end to the series, and I would've been okay if EoE was never released. Either way, EoE was amazing, but so was episodes 25 and 26.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Mar 19, 2010 12:30 am As with most episodes, 25 and 26 are really good, and even better than some of the others, especially better than those during the action arc. I suggest you watch them and formulate your own opinions on whether or not you like the aired episodes, or EoE. And if you're like me, who enjoys both, then watch both. It's not like those 45 minutes are going to kill you.

[]: Re: Should I bother watching the original ep25 and 26?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Mar 19, 2010 1:45 am CyberXIII wrote: Years later, I'm wondering: should I bother with the last two?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Mar 19, 2010 2:09 am I know I already commented, but I thought of something else to say to everyone. I really don't think you all would be saying EoTV was the better ending if EoE did not exist. When I watched through the first time back in 2001, I was like, "lol wtf, ok good thing there is the movie next." Anno trolled everyone pretty hard with EoTV. Without the more traditional storytelling of EoE, we would still be left guessing to what exactly happened with EoTV. That would have pissed just about all of us off to no end. I think EoTV is very important, but thank god that EoE exists.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Mar 19, 2010 2:41 am BrikHaus wrote: I think EoTV is very important, but thank god that EoE exists.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Mar 19, 2010 2:49 am Yes, you should definately watch the original, better ending. BrikHaus wrote: I really don't think you all would be saying EoTV was the better ending if EoE did not exist.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Mar 19, 2010 2:54 am BrikHaus wrote: When I watched through the first time back in 2001, I was like, "lol wtf, ok good thing there is the movie next." Anno trolled everyone pretty hard with EoTV. Without the more traditional storytelling of EoE, we would still be left guessing to what exactly happened with EoTV. That would have pissed just about all of us off to no end. I think EoTV is very important, but thank god that EoE exists.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Fri Mar 19, 2010 8:38 am I liked 25, but 26 was a little too ham-fistedly didactic. I think the second half of EoE does a better job dramatizing the show's themes. But definitely watch both.

[]: Re: Should I bother watching the original ep25 and 26?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Mar 19, 2010 9:06 am CyberXIII wrote: [I've never watched episodes 25 and 26.]

[]: Re: Should I bother watching the original ep25 and 26?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Fri Mar 19, 2010 9:46 am The Eva Monkey wrote: GET OFF MY PLANE

[]: Re: Should I bother watching the original ep25 and 26?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Mar 19, 2010 10:27 am You sir, have committed sacrilege. GTFO.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Fri Mar 19, 2010 4:14 pm All right, here's what I have to say: Both the TV ending and EoE are similar to each other, in that the events are taking place at the same time. When the TV ending was in production, it was supposed to be similar to what EoE ended up to be, but due to budget cuts and censorship, a different ending had to be made. Of course, after controversy amongst fans, the creator listened and then created EoE to satisfy the angry fans (some were death threats, and those are just Hardcore Anime fans). So, technically, both endings satisfy the creator. You might end up seeing the TV ending more than once to understand what the messages of the TV ending mean, though. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Fri Mar 19, 2010 6:28 pm Yes. I mean, watch it. Them. Love it, Hate it, Experience it. PS: ^ Actually, IIRC we only know 25' is similar to what was originally planned for 25.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Fri Mar 19, 2010 6:43 pm Watch them and get schooled in some heavy handed solipsism. Lili wrote: Love it, Hate it, Experience it.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Mar 19, 2010 9:08 pm Watch them. They are delicious.

[]:             Quote Post by paranoidandroid » Fri Mar 19, 2010 10:07 pm The congratulations at the end of 26 seems so much more light hearted than the the ending of EoE.
summary: []: Should I bother watching the original ep25 and 26?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Thu Mar 18, 2010 11:09 pm I watched the entire series in one go up to Kaworu's death. Now, I had spoiled myself silly, so I knew about most of the original series already, save for the last two episodes. However, after hearing about how bad they were, I instead decided to watch EoE instead, since it's evidently the real ending, more or less.
